zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Corbet I於大約1310年出生在英國史洛普爾華特萊斯波洛時，他的父親Thomas Corbet30歲，母親Amicia Hughes26歲。他於1337年與Mrs. Maud Corbet在英國英格蘭史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1367年之後在英國英格蘭史洛普爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Corbet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: History: Walter Corbet, a royal knight in 12th-century Scotland, was the first of that name in that kingdom. His 13th-century descendants were major landholders in the Borders, and in the 14th century they acquired lands in the North of Scotland.

variants.name: Corbett
meanings: English (West Midlands, of Norman origin): nickname from Old French corbet ‘raven’, probably denoting someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

History: This is the name of a family descended from Hugh Corbet, a Norman baron who settled in Shropshire following the Norman Conquest. One of his descendants, Sir Richard Corbet, was granted land near Shrewsbury in 1223; since the 13th century, this place has been known as Moreton Corbet. The name was taken from Shropshire to Scotland in the 12th century and to northern Ireland in the 17th century, and thence to North America by at least one group of bearers of the name.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When John de Corbet I was born about 1310, in Wattlesborough, Shropshire, England, his father, Thomas Corbet, was 30 and his mother, Amicia Hughes, was 26. He married Mrs. Maud Corbet in 1337, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died after 1367, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: John de Corbet I(이)가 대략 1310년 영국 슈럽실르 왓틀레스버루그h에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Corbet(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Amicia Hughes(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1337년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 Mrs. Maud Corbet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1367년 이후 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 사망했다.
